raw_text:
Confirmed.

That constraint (“tests must never spin up Docker”) is the right hard rule, and your bead split + assignments match it.

Assignments ✅

PurpleCliff → bd-1ez (purge phantom ignored stubs)

CopperRiver → bd-1oe (rewrite Phase10 governance tests to shared infra)

MaroonHarbor → bd-64k + bd-2hf (implement atomicity tests + CI lint guard)

Merge order (recommended)

bd-1ez first (removes fake coverage + clarifies remaining gaps)

bd-1oe next (removes Docker-in-test anti-pattern)

bd-64k then (adds real atomicity coverage)

bd-2hf last (so lint doesn’t block in-flight cleanup)

Stop-the-line checks

bd-1oe: enforce “no docker in tests” by deleting any Command::new("docker") usage and replacing with shared pools/clients or in-process routers.

bd-64k: if a flow is truly not callable without standing up extra infra, delete the test rather than leaving a stub; but for Payments webhook / Subscriptions cycle advance / GL reversal, prefer calling the real handler/service function directly within the test harness.

bd-2hf: lint should fail on any new #[ignore] unless the test is on an allowlist (target: only real_e2e.rs::test_real_nats_based_e2e, if you keep it).

Proceed.
